risk_description: Data inconsistency across microservices during financial transactions (e.g., Project Service status vs. Payment Service ledger) due to lack of atomic distributed transactions.
root_cause: Microservices architecture utilizing separate databases for Project and Payment services without a native distributed transaction coordinator.
mitigation_strategy: Implement the Saga Pattern for distributed transactions with compensating actions for rollback. Enforce eventual consistency checks via background reconciliation workers.
contingency_plan: Develop a manual 'Transaction Reconciliation' admin tool to force-correct ledger states based on Stripe source-of-truth.
monitoring_trigger: Detection of 'Orphaned Transaction' alerts where payment gateway confirms success but internal project status remains unchanged.

risk_description: AI SOW extraction (WI-SOW-002) producing inaccurate or hallucinated requirements, leading to incorrect Vendor Matching vectors and poor search relevance.
root_cause: Inherent non-deterministic nature of LLMs (OpenAI) when processing unstructured documents like SOWs.
mitigation_strategy: Mandate the 'Human-in-the-Loop' review interface (WI-SOW-003) as a blocking gate. Implement confidence scoring on extraction results to flag low-confidence fields.
contingency_plan: Fallback to manual data entry mode if AI service availability drops or error rates exceed 5%.
monitoring_trigger: User edit rate on AI-extracted fields > 30% during the review phase.

risk_description: Stripe Connect webhook failures or delays causing project status lock-ups, preventing 'Active' state transitions after payment.
root_cause: Reliance on asynchronous external webhooks for critical state transitions combined with potential network latency or signature verification failures.
mitigation_strategy: Implement robust webhook idempotency handling. Queue webhooks (SQS) for reliable processing with exponential backoff retries. Poll Stripe API as a backup if webhooks fail.
contingency_plan: Provide 'Refresh Payment Status' button in Admin UI to manually trigger an API pull from Stripe.
monitoring_trigger: Webhook processing Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) depth > 0.

risk_description: High cognitive load and context switching for development team due to polyglot stack (.NET, Python, TypeScript) and complex infrastructure.
root_cause: Architectural decision to use Python for AI, C# for Core API, and Node.js for Frontend/BFF, requiring distinct toolchains and expertise.
mitigation_strategy: Standardize containerization (Docker) and CI/CD pipelines across languages. Create unified 'developer experience' scripts. Assign service owners based on language expertise.
contingency_plan: Consolidate logic into .NET if Python/Node maintenance overhead becomes unsustainable.
monitoring_trigger: Sprint velocity volatility or increase in 'environment setup' support tickets.

risk_description: GDPR 'Right to be Forgotten' (US-076) conflicting with immutable audit log requirements (US-086), creating legal compliance exposure.
root_cause: Competing regulatory requirements: Data erasure (GDPR) vs. Financial/Security Audit trail retention (SOC 2).
mitigation_strategy: Implement 'Anonymization' rather than deletion for audit logs. Replace PII with unidentifiable UUIDs in audit records while hard-deleting the mapping table.
contingency_plan: Consult legal counsel to define exact 'Legal Hold' data retention periods that override GDPR deletion requests.
monitoring_trigger: Failed GDPR erasure jobs due to referential integrity constraints in the Audit Log.

risk_description: Performance bottlenecks in vector similarity search (pgvector) as the vendor database grows, violating the < 500ms latency requirement.
root_cause: Computational intensity of cosine similarity calculations on high-dimensional vectors without proper indexing (IVFFlat/HNSW).
mitigation_strategy: Implement HNSW indexing on the `embedding` column in PostgreSQL. Ensure RDS instance is provisioned with sufficient RAM to keep the index in memory.
contingency_plan: Migrate vector search workload to a dedicated OpenSearch cluster if PostgreSQL performance degrades.

risk_description: MFA setup friction or loss of recovery codes locking out Admin/Finance users, halting critical business operations.
root_cause: Strict enforcement of MFA for privileged roles without a defined 'Break Glass' procedure.
mitigation_strategy: Establish a secure 'Break Glass' super-admin account with credentials stored in a physical safe/vault. Implement rigorous recovery code download prompts (US-008).
contingency_plan: Manual identity verification procedure via AWS Support/Root account holder to reset MFA for locked admins.
monitoring_trigger: Support tickets tagged 'Account Lockout' > 2 per week.
